{"step_by_step_thinking": "Ameloblastoma is a rare odontogenic tumor that arises from the epithelium of the tooth root, primarily in the jaw, not in the brain. The retrieved documents confirm that ameloblastomas are most commonly found in the posterior mandible and maxilla, and are benign but locally aggressive. They do not occur in the brain. The documents also state that ameloblastomas are rare, representing about 1% of all jaw tumors, and are not associated with the central nervous system. There is no mention of ameloblastoma occurring in the brain. Therefore, it is not a common benign tumor occurring in the brain.", "answer_choice": "B"}
